input.content:
You are an AI assistant tasked with solving command-line tasks in a Linux environment. You will be given a task description and the output from previously executed commands. Your goal is to solve the task by providing batches of shell commands.

Format your response as JSON with the following structure:

{
  "analysis": "Analyze the current state based on the terminal output provided. What do you see? What has been accomplished? What still needs to be done?",
  "plan": "Describe your plan for the next steps. What commands will you run and why? Be specific about what you expect each command to accomplish.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "ls -la\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "cd project\n",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": true
}

Required fields:
- "analysis": Your analysis of the current situation
- "plan": Your plan for the next steps
- "commands": Array of command objects to execute

Optional fields:
- "task_complete": Boolean indicating if the task is complete (defaults to false if not present)

Command object structure:
- "keystrokes": String containing the exact keystrokes to send to the terminal (required)
- "duration": Number of seconds to wait for the command to complete before the next command will be executed (defaults to 1.0 if not present)

IMPORTANT: The text inside "keystrokes" will be used completely verbatim as keystrokes. Write commands exactly as you want them sent to the terminal:
- You must end every command with a newline (\n) or it will not execute.
- For special key sequences, use tmux-style escape sequences:
  - C-c for Ctrl+C
  - C-d for Ctrl+D

The "duration" attribute specifies the number of seconds to wait for the command to complete (default: 1.0) before the next command will be executed. On immediate tasks (e.g., cd, ls, echo, cat) set a duration of 0.1 seconds. On commands (e.g., gcc, find, rustc) set a duration of 1.0 seconds. On slow commands (e.g., make, python3 [long running script], wget [file]) set an appropriate duration as you determine necessary.

It is better to set a smaller duration than a longer duration. It is always possible to wait again if the prior output has not finished, by running {"keystrokes": "", "duration": 10.0} on subsequent requests to wait longer. Never wait longer than 60 seconds; prefer to poll to see intermediate result status.

Important notes:
- Each command's keystrokes are sent exactly as written to the terminal
- Do not include extra whitespace before or after the keystrokes unless it's part of the intended command
- Extra text before or after the JSON will generate warnings but be tolerated
- The JSON must be valid - use proper escaping for quotes and special characters within strings
- Commands array can be empty if you want to wait without taking action

Task Description:
The project in `/app` is a local plugin-style runtime with Node.js orchestration and a Rust helper invoked through `cargo run`. Repeated local runs should behave like one uninterrupted session: carried state stays consistent, reloads do not lose pending work, dependency changes stay isolated to the records that use them, and older records remain readable. Right now the report drifts depending on the run sequence.

Run `npm run matrix` from `/app`. The scenario runner writes `/app/outcome/report.json` with per-scenario records and a folded aggregate. Each record carries its scenario name, ordered rows, a closing finalCounter, dependencyTotal, acceptedOlder, and healthy flag. Each row exposes index, value, factor, nativeValue, and a sorted deferred list. The aggregate reports source, recordCount, totalCounter, dependencyTotal, rowTotal, healthyCount, and status.

Each cycle's native row feeds the next cycle, and a scenario's closing counter should reflect the last native row produced—not merely the last input base. Older native revision markers must normalize like newer ones, incorporating carried state into the native row. Deferred numbers listed on a cycle belong to that same cycle's row, not the next. Descriptor factors are recomputed per row from that row's descriptor number and position, without cross-row caching. A scenario is healthy when older records are accepted and its closing counter equals the sum of input bases. Aggregate status reads healthy when every scenario is healthy, otherwise stale. Counters, dependency totals, row totals, and status must be derived from the records, not separate fixtures.

Repair bugs in the existing Node modules and Rust crate. Do not replace the runner with hardcoded JSON or a single-script reimplementation that skips cargo run. Invoking cargo run compiles the native helper under `/app/target`.


Current terminal state:
Current Terminal Screen:
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app#

New Terminal Output:
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app# cd /app
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app# npm run matrix

> local-runtime-matrix@1.0.0 matrix
> node tools/run_matrix.js

/app/a1/p.js:11
    throw new Error(proc.stderr || proc.stdout || 'native worker failed');
    ^

Error: native worker failed
    at callNative (/app/a1/p.js:11:11)
    at f_a (/app/a1/p.js:24:25)
    at /app/tools/run_matrix.js:14:49
    at Array.map (<anonymous>)
    at main (/app/tools/run_matrix.js:14:34)
    at Object.<anonymous> (/app/tools/run_matrix.js:20:1)
    at Module._compile (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1364:14)
    at Module._extensions..js (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1422:10)
    at Module.load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1203:32)
    at Module._load (node:internal/modules/cjs/loader:1019:12)

Node.js v18.20.4
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app# ls -la outcome
ls: cannot access 'outcome': No such file or directory
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app# jq '.' outcome/report.json | head -200
bash: jq: command not found
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app# jq '{aggregate:.aggregate, records:(.records|map({scenario,finalCounter,dependencyTotal,acceptedOlder,healthy,rowsCount:(.rows|length)})
)}' outcome/report.json
bash: jq: command not found
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app# rg -n "matrix|report\.json|outcome|scenario runner|acceptedOlder|finalCounter|dependencyTotal|deferred|nativeValue|factor|descriptor|car
go run" .
bash: rg: command not found
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app# ls -la
total 20
drwxr-xr-x 12 root root 225 May 25 12:15 .
drwxr-xr-x 19 root root 265 May 25 12:16 ..
-rw-r--r--  1 root root 114 May 25 12:12 Cargo.lock
-rw-r--r--  1 root root 166 May 25 12:12 Cargo.toml
-rw-r--r--  1 root root 540 May 25 12:12 README.md
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  18 May 25 12:12 a1

drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  98 May 25 12:12 docs
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  28 May 25 12:12 fixtures

-rw-r--r--  1 root root 204 May 25 12:12 package.json
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  28 May 25 12:15 scripts
drwxr-xr-x  4 root root  32 May 25 12:12 src
drwxr-xr-x  3 root root  63 May 25 12:15 target
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root  27 May 25 12:15 tools
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app# cat package.json
{"name":"local-runtime-matrix","version":"1.0.0","private":true,"description":"local mixed runtime scenario matrix","scripts":{"matrix":"node tools/run_matrix.j
s"},"dependencies":{},"devDependencies":{}}
root@eca5a5f6d31b:/app#
